# Eduentry

> Eduentry is a free AI-powered adaptive assessment platform that measures how children aged 6–17 perform against international education standards, providing percentile rankings and standardised scores.

Eduentry uses 2PL Item Response Theory (IRT) with MAP estimation — the same methodology as PISA, GCSE, and professional educational psychologists — to produce standardised scores (mean 100, SD 15) across four subjects. Results are benchmarked against four international frameworks to give families a global context for their child's academic standing.

## What Eduentry does

- Adaptive academic assessment for children aged 6–17
- Four subjects: English, Mathematics, Verbal Reasoning, Non-Verbal Reasoning
- 15 questions per subject (60 total), adapting in real time to the child's ability level
- Standardised scores with percentile rankings on a mean-100, SD-15 scale
- International benchmarking against: UK National Curriculum, US grade levels, PISA (OECD), IB Programme
- AI-generated personalised study recommendations per subject
- Free to use — no subscription required

## Score interpretation

- Below 85: Needs Support (below -1 SD)
- 85–94: Below Average (-1 SD to -0.33 SD)
- 95–109: Average (within ±0.33 SD of mean)
- 110–119: Above Average (+0.67 SD to +1.33 SD)
- 120 and above: Exceptional (above +1.33 SD)

## International benchmarks

Scores map to:
- **UK National Curriculum**: below expected standard / approaching / meeting / above / highly able
- **US grade levels**: below / approaching / at / above / significantly above grade level
- **PISA proficiency**: Levels 2 through 6 (OECD scale)
- **IB Programme**: Foundation / MYP / Diploma Standard Level / Diploma Higher Level readiness

## Methodology

The assessment uses 2-Parameter Logistic (2PL) IRT with Maximum A Posteriori (MAP) estimation. Each question is generated by AI and calibrated to a difficulty and discrimination parameter. The adaptive engine selects questions to maximise information at the child's estimated ability level. Final scores are reported on a standardised scale (mean=100, SD=15) equivalent to the scale used by professional cognitive assessments such as CAT4 and the 11+ standardised tests.

Note: question content is AI-generated and not empirically calibrated with large normative populations. Results are indicative, not diagnostic.

## Target audience

- Parents of children aged 6–17 in the UK, internationally, and expatriate families
- Families preparing for 11+, grammar school entry, selective independent school admission
- Families considering IB schools or international school admission
- Parents seeking to understand how their child compares globally (not just locally)

## Website

- Homepage: https://eduentry.com
- Free assessment: https://eduentry.com (requires account creation)
- Sitemap: https://eduentry.com/sitemap.xml
